Це звук як англійська літера "j". Think of "jungle". **ДЖ** is the voiced partner of **Ч**.

- **джерело́** (spring, source) — a beautiful word.
- **бджола́** (bee) — notice the **Б** before the digraph.

> [!tip] **Remember the pair**
> **Ч** (глухий) ↔ **ДЖ** (дзвінкий). Just like English «ch» ↔ «j.»

### ДЗ

**ДЗ** — унікальний звук. It has no equivalent in English. It is the voiced partner of **Ц**. Think of **Ц** (ts). Then add voicing. Your vocal cords vibrate through the whole sound.

- **дзвін** (bell) — the sound of church bells.
- **дзе́ркало** (mirror) — an everyday object.

> [!culture] **Uniquely Ukrainian**
> Диграф **ДЗ** — це особливість фонетики. The word **дзвін** captures something deeply cultural. It is the sound of church bells across Ukrainian towns. **ДЗ** — це особливий звук.

Now you know all the letters. Here is the complete 33-letter alphabet:

**А Б В Г Ґ Д Е Є Ж З И І Ї Й К Л М Н О П Р С Т У Ф Х Ц Ч Ш Щ Ь Ю Я**

> [!challenge] **Full-alphabet reading challenge**
> Try to read this text. It uses everything you have learned:

- **Це сім'я́ і м'яч.** (This is a family and a ball.)
- **Джерело́ — це щастя.** (A spring is happiness.)
- **Дзвін — це факт.** (A bell is a fact.)
- **Кінь і бджола́.** (A horse and a bee.)
- **О́сінь. Цу́кор і сіль.** (Autumn. Sugar and salt.)

You did it! You did it! Every word above uses the letters from this module. If you can read them, you can decode any word.

Ось п'ять важливих фраз. Practice reading them aloud:

- **До́брий день!** (Good day!)
- **Як спра́ви?** (How are you?)
- **До́брий ра́нок!** (Good morning!)
- **До́брий ве́чір!** (Good evening!)
- **До поба́чення!** (Goodbye!)

> [!tip] **Фінал! — Celebrate!**
> You can read any word. The reading skills you built are the foundation. From here on, every module will teach you more. Чудова робота! Great job!

## Підсумок — Summary

У цьому модулі ви завершили алфавіт:

- **Ь** (м'який знак) — пом'якшує приголосний. Слова: **сіль**, **день**, **кінь**.
- **Апостроф** (**'**) — розділяє приголосний та голосну. Слова: **м'ясо**, **п'ять**, **сім'я**.
- **Ц, Ч, Щ** — африкати. Слова: **цукор**, **час**, **що**.
- **Ф** — звучить як англійське «f». Слова: **факт**, **фото**.
- **ДЖ, ДЗ** — диграфи. Слова: **джерело**, **дзвін**.

**Self-check — can you answer these?**

1. Що робить м'який знак (Ь)?
2. Після яких приголосних стоїть апостроф?
3. Які звуки утворюють літеру **Щ**?
4. Чому **ДЗ** унікальний?

If you can answer all four, you are ready for Module 5. There you will learn about syllables.

toolCalls.description:
Replaces text within a file. By default, the tool expects to find and replace exactly ONE occurrence of `old_string`. If you want to replace multiple occurrences of the exact same string, set `allow_multiple` to true. This tool requires providing significant context around the change to ensure precise targeting. Always use the read_file tool to examine the file's current content before attempting a text replacement.
      
      The user has the ability to modify the `new_string` content. If modified, this will be stated in the response.
      
      Expectation for required parameters:
      1. `old_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace (including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.).
      2. `new_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace `old_string` with (also including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.). Ensure the resulting code is correct and idiomatic and that `old_string` and `new_string` are different.
      3. `instruction` is the detailed instruction of what needs to be changed. It is important to Make it specific and detailed so developers or large language models can understand what needs to be changed and perform the changes on their own if necessary. 
      4. NEVER escape `old_string` or `new_string`, that would break the exact literal text requirement.
      **Important:** If ANY of the above are not satisfied, the tool will fail. CRITICAL for `old_string`: Must uniquely identify the instance(s) to change. Include at least 3 lines of context BEFORE and AFTER the target text, matching whitespace and indentation precisely. If this string matches multiple locations and `allow_multiple` is not true, the tool will fail.
      5. Prefer to break down complex and long changes into multiple smaller atomic calls to this tool. Always check the content of the file after changes or not finding a string to match.
      **Multiple replacements:** Set `allow_multiple` to true if you want to replace ALL occurrences that match `old_string` exactly.

Це звук як англійська літера "j". Think of "jungle". **ДЖ** is the voiced partner of **Ч**.

- **джерело́** (spring, source) — a beautiful word.
- **бджола́** (bee) — notice the **Б** before the digraph.

> [!tip] **Remember the pair**
> **Ч** (глухий) ↔ **ДЖ** (дзвінкий). Just like English «ch» ↔ «j.»

### ДЗ

**ДЗ** — унікальний звук. It has no equivalent in English. It is the voiced partner of **Ц**. Think of **Ц** (ts). Then add voicing. Your vocal cords vibrate through the whole sound.

- **дзвін** (bell) — the sound of church bells.
- **дзе́ркало** (mirror) — an everyday object.

> [!culture] **Uniquely Ukrainian**
> Диграф **ДЗ** — це особливість фонетики. The word **дзвін** captures something deeply cultural. It is the sound of church bells across Ukrainian towns. **ДЗ** — це особливий звук.

## Весь алфавіт! — The Full Alphabet Mastered

Now you know all the letters. Here is the complete 33-letter alphabet:

**А Б В Г Ґ Д Е Є Ж З И І Ї Й К Л М Н О П Р С Т У Ф Х Ц Ч Ш Щ Ь Ю Я**

А також диграфи **ДЖ**, **ДЗ** та **апостроф** (**'**).

> [!challenge] **Full-alphabet reading challenge**
> Try to read this text. It uses everything you have learned:

- **Це сім'я́ і м'яч.** (This is a family and a ball.)
- **Джерело́ — це щастя.** (A spring is happiness.)
- **Дзвін — це факт.** (A bell is a fact.)
- **Кінь і бджола́.** (A horse and a bee.)
- **О́сінь. Цу́кор і сіль.** (Autumn. Sugar and salt.)

You did it! You did it! Every word above uses the letters from this module. If you can read them, you can decode any word.

Ось п'ять важливих фраз. Practice reading them aloud:

- **До́брий день!** (Good day!)
- **Як спра́ви?** (How are you?)
- **До́брий ра́нок!** (Good morning!)
- **До́брий ве́чір!** (Good evening!)
- **До поба́чення!** (Goodbye!)

> [!tip] **Фінал! — Celebrate!**
> You can read any word. The reading skills you built are the foundation. From here on, every module will teach you more. Чудова робота! Great job!

## Підсумок — Summary

У цьому модулі ви завершили алфавіт:

- **Ь** (м'який знак) — пом'якшує приголосний. Слова: **сіль**, **день**, **кінь**.
- **Апостроф** (**'**) — розділяє приголосний та голосну. Слова: **м'ясо**, **п'ять**, **сім'я**.
- **Ц, Ч, Щ** — африкати. Слова: **цукор**, **час**, **що**.
- **Ф** — звучить як англійське «f». Слова: **факт**, **фото**.
- **ДЖ, ДЗ** — диграфи. Слова: **джерело**, **дзвін**.

**Self-check — can you answer these?**

1. Що робить м'який знак (Ь)?
2. Після яких приголосних стоїть апостроф?
3. Які звуки утворюють літеру **Щ**?
4. Чому **ДЗ** унікальний?

If you can answer all four, you are ready for Module 5. There you will learn about syllables.

## Вступ — Introduction

Це великий крок. This is a big step. In Module 1, we mapped out all 33 letters. Module 2 was about vowels. Module 3 focused on consonants. Now, we are ready for the final pieces of the puzzle.

Today you will meet two special symbols. The soft sign **Ь** and the **apostrophe** (**'**). Далі — африкати **Ц**, **Ч**, **Щ**. Next are the affricates **Ц**, **Ч**, **Щ**. These letters pack two sounds into one. Then come the digraphs **ДЖ** and **ДЗ**. These are single sounds written with two letters. Finally, there is the rare letter **Ф**.

Це фінал. This is the finale. After this module, you will be able to read *any* Ukrainian word. Every letter, every symbol, every combination. You will know them all. Let's finish what we started.

## М'який знак — The Soft Sign

Imagine a letter with no sound of its own. The letter **Ь** is exactly that. It only exists to soften (palatalize) the consonant before it. With the letter **Ь**, place your tongue closer to the roof of your mouth. The result is a gentler, «softer» version of the same sound.

Ось кілька слів із **Ь**:

- **сіль** (salt) — the **Л** is soft.
- **де́нь** (day) — the **Н** is soft.
- **Льві́в** (Lviv) — the **Л** is soft before another consonant.
- **мідь** (copper) — the **Д** is soft.
- **о́сінь** (autumn) — the **Н** is soft.
- **кінь** (horse) — the **Н** is soft.

Look at the rules. **Ь** appears after consonants at the end of a word (**сіль**, **день**). It also appears before another consonant (**Львів**). It never appears at the beginning of a word. It never appears after a vowel.

> [!tip] **Quick win!**
> Here is the greeting **Добрий день!** (Good day!). Now you see why there is a **Ь** at the end. The **Н** is soft. Try to pronounce this. The tip of your tongue touches your palate.

Чому **Ь** важливий? Why is **Ь** important? Look at this minimal pair:

- **кінь** (horse) — soft **Н** at the end.
- **кін** (a stake in a game) — hard **Н** at the end.

Однакові літери, але різне значення. Same letters, but different meaning. The **Ь** changes the quality of the consonant. This creates an entirely different word. Це не просто прикраса. This is not decoration. It is essential.

<!-- adapted from: Kravtsova, Grade 3 — minimal pair exercises for Ь -->

## Апостроф — The Apostrophe

Маленький, але сильний знак. A small but powerful mark. The **apostrophe** (**'**) separates a consonant from a vowel (**Я**, **Ю**, **Є**, **Ї**). This preserves the **Й**-sound. Without it, the sound would be absorbed.

Remember from Module 2. Iotated vowels carry a hidden **Й** at the start: **Я** = Й+А, **Ю** = Й+У, **Є** = Й+Е, **Ї** = Й+І. The apostrophe keeps that **Й** alive.

Ось слова, де є апостроф:

- **м'я́со** (meat) — hard **М** + Й + А.
- **п'ять** (five) — hard **П** + Й + А.
- **сім'я́** (family) — hard **М** + Й + А.
- **м'яч** (ball) — hard **М** + Й + А.
- **об'є́кт** (object) — hard **Б** + Й + Е.

> [!note] **Правило апострофа — The apostrophe rule**
> Апостроф — після приголосних **Б**, **П**, **В**, **М**, **Ф**, **Р**. Апостроф — перед **Я**, **Ю**, **Є**, **Ї**. If you see one of these consonants before an iotated vowel, look for the apostrophe.

Чому це важливо? Compare what happens without it versus with it:

- Without it: **М + Я** means «soft М followed by А». The **Й** disappears.
- With it: **М'Я** means «hard М + Й + А». The **Й** is preserved.

Апостроф — це не опція. The apostrophe is not optional. Writing **мясо** instead of **м'ясо** changes the pronunciation entirely. Ukrainian school textbooks drill this from Grade 2. Now you know it too.

<!-- adapted from: Bolshakova, Grade 2 — apostrophe exercises p. 57-58 -->

> [!tip] **Kitchen connection**
> Notice this: **м'ясо** (meat), **сіль** (salt), and **цу́кор** (sugar) are kitchen words. Ukrainian food vocabulary is a great way to practice these symbols.

## Африкати та Ф — Affricates and Ф

Африкати — це особливі приголосні. Affricates are special consonants. They fuse two sounds into one. Your mouth starts in one position. It quickly shifts to another. This happens within a single letter.

### Літера Ц

Start with the sound **Т**. Smoothly transition to **С**. **Ц** fuses them into one sound. Think of English «cats». That is the sound of **Ц**. Your tongue touches the ridge behind your upper teeth. Then it quickly releases into an «s.»

- **цу́кор** (sugar) — an everyday kitchen word.
- **цибу́ля** (onion) — another kitchen staple.

You will often see **Ц** in endings: **-ець**, **-иця**. It is a very productive letter.

### Літера Ч

Схожа на англійський звук "ch". Think of "church". **Ч** is one of the most frequent consonants in Ukrainian.

- **час** (time, hour) — a top 100 word.
- **черепа́ха** (turtle) — a favorite in children's books.
- **чай** (tea) — you will hear this word every day.

> [!practice] **Sound it out**
> Try to pronounce these words. For **Ч**, your tongue presses against the roof. Then it releases with a burst of air.

### Літера Щ

Унікальна українська літера. It contains two sounds: **Ш** + **Ч**. It is uniquely Ukrainian in its pronunciation. Start with the sound «ш». Immediately add «ч.» The result is longer than a simple «sh.»

- **що** (what) — одне з найпопулярніших слів.
- **ще** (still, more) — very common.
- **ща́стя** (happiness) — a beautiful word.

Слово **що** є майже в кожній розмові. You will meet it again and again. Getting its pronunciation right now is a great investment.

> [!warning] **Common mistake**
> Do not replace **Щ** with the sound «ш.» Saying **шо** instead of **що** is a common error. Remember: **Щ** = **Ш** + **Ч**. These are two sounds in one letter.

### Літера Ф

Найпростіша літера в цьому модулі. **Ф** sounds just like English «f.» But it is rare in native Ukrainian words. Most words with **Ф** are borrowings:

- **факт** (fact) — an internationalism.
- **фо́то** (photo) — another familiar borrowing.

**Ф** — це глухий партнер літери **В**. Native Ukrainian words historically preferred **В** or **ХВ**. You will not see **Ф** often. But you should recognize it instantly.

## Диграфи ДЖ, ДЗ — Digraphs

В українській мові є два диграфи. There are two digraphs. These are combinations of two letters for one sound. Do not read them as separate letters. Each digraph is one phoneme.

Це звук як англійська літера "j". Think of "jungle". **ДЖ** is the voiced partner of **Ч**.

- **бджола́** (bee) — notice the **Б** before the digraph.

> [!tip] **Remember the pair**
> **Ч** (глухий) ↔ **ДЖ** (дзвінкий). Just like English «ch» ↔ «j.»

### ДЗ

**ДЗ** — унікальний звук. It has no equivalent in English. It is the voiced partner of **Ц**. Think of **Ц** (ts). Then add voicing. Your vocal cords vibrate through the whole sound.

- **дзвін** (bell) — the sound of church bells.
- **дзе́ркало** (mirror) — an everyday object.

> [!culture] **Uniquely Ukrainian**
> Диграф **ДЗ** — це особливість фонетики. The word **дзвін** captures something deeply cultural. It is the sound of church bells across Ukrainian towns. **ДЗ** — це особливий звук.

## Весь алфавіт! — The Full Alphabet Mastered

Now you know all the letters. Here is the complete 33-letter alphabet:

**А Б В Г Ґ Д Е Є Ж З И І Ї Й К Л М Н О П Р С Т У Ф Х Ц Ч Ш Щ Ь Ю Я**

А також диграфи **ДЖ**, **ДЗ** та **апостроф** (**'**).

> [!challenge] **Full-alphabet reading challenge**

- **Це сім'я́ і м'яч.** (This is a family and a ball.)
- **Джерело́ — це щастя.** (A spring is happiness.)
- **Дзвін — це факт.** (A bell is a fact.)
- **Кінь і бджола́.** (A horse and a bee.)
- **О́сінь. Цу́кор і сіль.** (Autumn. Sugar and salt.)

You did it! You did it! Every word above uses the letters from this module. If you can read them, you can decode any word.

Ось п'ять важливих фраз. Practice reading them aloud:

- **До́брий день!** (Good day!)
- **Як спра́ви?** (How are you?)
- **До́брий ра́нок!** (Good morning!)
- **До́брий ве́чір!** (Good evening!)
- **До поба́чення!** (Goodbye!)

> [!tip] **Фінал! — Celebrate!**
> You can read any word. The reading skills you built are the foundation. From here on, every module will teach you more. Чудова робота! Great job!

## Підсумок — Summary

У цьому модулі — фінал алфавіту:

- **Ь** (м'який знак) — пом'якшує приголосний. Слова: **сіль**, **день**, **кінь**.

4. Чому **ДЗ** унікальний?

Replaces text within a file. By default, the tool expects to find and replace exactly ONE occurrence of `old_string`. If you want to replace multiple occurrences of the exact same string, set `allow_multiple` to true. This tool requires providing significant context around the change to ensure precise targeting. Always use the read_file tool to examine the file's current content before attempting a text replacement.
      
      The user has the ability to modify the `new_string` content. If modified, this will be stated in the response.
      
      Expectation for required parameters:
      1. `old_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace (including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.).
      2. `new_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace `old_string` with (also including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.). Ensure the resulting code is correct and idiomatic and that `old_string` and `new_string` are different.
      3. `instruction` is the detailed instruction of what needs to be changed. It is important to Make it specific and detailed so developers or large language models can understand what needs to be changed and perform the changes on their own if necessary. 
      4. NEVER escape `old_string` or `new_string`, that would break the exact literal text requirement.
      **Important:** If ANY of the above are not satisfied, the tool will fail. CRITICAL for `old_string`: Must uniquely identify the instance(s) to change. Include at least 3 lines of context BEFORE and AFTER the target text, matching whitespace and indentation precisely. If this string matches multiple locations and `allow_multiple` is not true, the tool will fail.
      5. Prefer to break down complex and long changes into multiple smaller atomic calls to this tool. Always check the content of the file after changes or not finding a string to match.
      **Multiple replacements:** Set `allow_multiple` to true if you want to replace ALL occurrences that match `old_string` exactly.

- **о́сінь** (autumn) — the **Н** is soft.

<!-- adapted from: Kravtsova, Grade 3 — minimal pair exercises for Ь -->

## Апостроф — The Apostrophe

Маленький, але сильний знак. A small but powerful mark. The **apostrophe** (**'**) separates a consonant from a vowel (**Я**, **Ю**, **Є**, **Ї**). This preserves the **Й**-sound. Without it, the sound would be absorbed.

- **м'я́со** (meat) — hard **М** + Й + А.
- **п'ять** (five) — hard **П** + Й + А.
- **сім'я́** (family) — hard **М** + Й + А.
- **м'яч** (ball) — hard **М** + Й + А.
- **об'є́кт** (object) — hard **Б** + Й + Е.

> [!note] **Правило апострофа — The apostrophe rule**
> Апостроф — після приголосних **Б**, **П**, **В**, **М**, **Ф**, **Р**. Апостроф — перед **Я**, **Ю**, **Є**, **Ї**. If you see one of these consonants before an iotated vowel, look for the apostrophe.

Чому це важливо? Compare what happens without it versus with it:

- Without it: **М + Я** means «soft М followed by А». The **Й** disappears.

## Африкати та Ф — Affricates and Ф

Африкати — це особливі приголосні. Affricates are special consonants. They fuse two sounds into one. Your mouth starts in one position. It quickly shifts to another. This happens within a single letter.

### Літера Ц

- **цибу́ля** (onion) — another kitchen staple.

You will often see **Ц** in endings: **-ець**, **-иця**. It is a very productive letter.

### Літера Ч

Схожа на англійський звук "ch". Think of "church". **Ч** is one of the most frequent consonants in Ukrainian.

- **час** (time, hour) — a top 100 word.
- **черепа́ха** (turtle) — a favorite in children's books.
- **чай** (tea) — you will hear this word every day.

> [!practice] **Sound it out**
> Try to pronounce these words. For **Ч**, your tongue presses against the roof. Then it releases with a burst of air.

### Літера Щ

Унікальна українська літера. It contains two sounds: **Ш** + **Ч**. It is uniquely Ukrainian in its pronunciation. Start with the sound «ш». Immediately add «ч.» The result is longer than a simple «sh.»

- **що** (what) — одне з найпопулярніших слів.
- **ще** (still, more) — very common.
- **ща́стя** (happiness) — a beautiful word.

Слово **що** є майже в кожній розмові. You will meet it again and again. Getting its pronunciation right now is a great investment.

> [!warning] **Common mistake**
> Do not replace **Щ** with the sound «ш.» Saying **шо** instead of **що** is a common error. Remember: **Щ** = **Ш** + **Ч**. These are two sounds in one letter.

### Літера Ф

Найпростіша літера в цьому модулі. **Ф** sounds just like English «f.» But it is rare in native Ukrainian words. Most words with **Ф** are borrowings:

- **факт** (fact) — an internationalism.
- **фо́то** (photo) — another familiar borrowing.

**Ф** — це глухий партнер літери **В**. Native Ukrainian words historically preferred **В** or **ХВ**. You will not see **Ф** often. But you should recognize it instantly.

## Диграфи ДЖ, ДЗ — Digraphs

В українській мові є два диграфи. There are two digraphs. These are combinations of two letters for one sound. Do not read them as separate letters. Each digraph is one phoneme.

### ДЖ

Це звук як англійська літера "j". Think of "jungle". **ДЖ** is the voiced partner of **Ч**.

- **джерело́** (spring, source) — a beautiful word.
- **бджола́** (bee) — notice the **Б** before the digraph.

> [!tip] **Remember the pair**
> **Ч** (глухий) ↔ **ДЖ** (дзвінкий). Just like English «ch» ↔ «j.»

### ДЗ

**ДЗ** — унікальний звук. It has no equivalent in English. It is the voiced partner of **Ц**. Think of **Ц** (ts). Then add voicing. Your vocal cords vibrate through the whole sound.

- **дзвін** (bell) — the sound of church bells.
- **дзе́ркало** (mirror) — an everyday object.

> [!culture] **Uniquely Ukrainian**
> Диграф **ДЗ** — це особливість фонетики. The word **дзвін** captures something deeply cultural. It is the sound of church bells across Ukrainian towns. **ДЗ** — це особливий звук.

## Весь алфавіт! — The Full Alphabet Mastered

Now you know all the letters. Here is the complete 33-letter alphabet:

**А Б В Г Ґ Д Е Є Ж З И І Ї Й К Л М Н О П Р С Т У Ф Х Ц Ч Ш Щ Ь Ю Я**

А також диграфи **ДЖ**, **ДЗ** та **апостроф** (**'**).

> [!challenge] **Full-alphabet reading challenge**
> Try to read this text. It uses everything you have learned:

- **Це сім'я́ і м'яч.** (This is a family and a ball.)
- **Джерело́ — це щастя.** (A spring is happiness.)
- **Дзвін — це факт.** (A bell is a fact.)
- **Кінь і бджола́.** (A horse and a bee.)
- **О́сінь. Цу́кор і сіль.** (Autumn. Sugar and salt.)

You did it! You did it! Every word above uses the letters from this module. If you can read them, you can decode any word.

Ось п'ять важливих фраз. Practice reading them aloud:

- **До́брий день!** (Good day!)
- **Як спра́ви?** (How are you?)
- **До́брий ра́нок!** (Good morning!)
- **До́брий ве́чір!** (Good evening!)
- **До поба́чення!** (Goodbye!)

> [!tip] **Фінал! — Celebrate!**
> You can read any word. The reading skills you built are the foundation. From here on, every module will teach you more. Чудова робота! Great job!

## Підсумок — Summary

У цьому модулі — фінал алфавіту:

- **Ь** (м'який знак) — пом'якшує приголосний. Слова: **сіль**, **день**, **кінь**.
- **Апостроф** (**'**) — розділяє приголосний та голосну. Слова: **м'ясо**, **п'ять**, **сім'я**.
- **Ц, Ч, Щ** — африкати. Слова: **цукор**, **час**, **що**.
- **Ф** — звучить як англійське «f». Слова: **факт**, **фото**.
- **ДЖ, ДЗ** — диграфи. Слова: **джерело**, **дзвін**.

**Self-check — can you answer these?**

1. Що робить м'який знак (Ь)?
2. Після яких приголосних стоїть апостроф?
3. Які звуки утворюють літеру **Щ**?
4. Чому **ДЗ** унікальний?

If you can answer all four, you are ready for Module 5. There you will learn about syllables.

Це фінал. This is the finale. After this module, you will be able to read *any* Ukrainian word. Every letter, every symbol, every combination. You will know them all. Let's finish what we started.

## М'який знак — The Soft Sign

Imagine a letter with no sound of its own. The letter **Ь** is exactly that. It only exists to soften (palatalize) the consonant before it. With the letter **Ь**, place your tongue closer to the roof of your mouth. The result is a gentler, «softer» version of the same sound.

Ось кілька слів із **Ь**:

- **сіль** (salt) — the **Л** is soft.
- **де́нь** (day) — the **Н** is soft.
- **Льві́в** (Lviv) — the **Л** is soft before another consonant.
- **мідь** (copper) — the **Д** is soft.
- **о́сінь** (autumn) — the **Н** is soft.
- **кінь** (horse) — the **Н** is soft.

Look at the rules. **Ь** appears after consonants at the end of a word (**сіль**, **день**). It also appears before another consonant (**Львів**). It never appears at the beginning of a word. It never appears after a vowel.

> [!tip] **Quick win!**
> Here is the greeting **Добрий день!** (Good day!). Now you see why there is a **Ь** at the end. The **Н** is soft. Try to pronounce this. The tip of your tongue touches your palate.

Чому **Ь** важливий? Why is **Ь** important? Look at this minimal pair:

- **кінь** (horse) — soft **Н** at the end.
- **кін** (a stake in a game) — hard **Н** at the end.

Однакові літери, але різне значення. Same letters, but different meaning. The **Ь** changes the quality of the consonant. This creates an entirely different word. Це не просто прикраса. This is not decoration. It is essential.

<!-- adapted from: Kravtsova, Grade 3 — minimal pair exercises for Ь -->

## Апостроф — The Apostrophe

Маленький, але сильний знак. A small but powerful mark. The **apostrophe** (**'**) separates a consonant from a vowel (**Я**, **Ю**, **Є**, **Ї**). This preserves the **Й**-sound. Without it, the sound would be absorbed.

Remember from Module 2. Iotated vowels carry a hidden **Й** at the start: **Я** = Й+А, **Ю** = Й+У, **Є** = Й+Е, **Ї** = Й+І. The apostrophe keeps that **Й** alive.

Ось слова, де є апостроф:

- **м'я́со** (meat) — hard **М** + Й + А.
- **п'ять** (five) — hard **П** + Й + А.
- **сім'я́** (family) — hard **М** + Й + А.
- **м'яч** (ball) — hard **М** + Й + А.
- **об'є́кт** (object) — hard **Б** + Й + Е.

> [!note] **Правило апострофа — The apostrophe rule**
> Апостроф — після приголосних **Б**, **П**, **В**, **М**, **Ф**, **Р**. Апостроф — перед **Я**, **Ю**, **Є**, **Ї**. If you see one of these consonants before an iotated vowel, look for the apostrophe.

Чому це важливо? Compare what happens without it versus with it:

- Without it: **М + Я** means «soft М followed by А». The **Й** disappears.
- With it: **М'Я** means «hard М + Й + А». The **Й** is preserved.

Апостроф — це не опція. The apostrophe is not optional. Writing **мясо** instead of **м'ясо** changes the pronunciation entirely. Ukrainian school textbooks drill this from Grade 2. Now you know it too.

> Notice this: **м'ясо** (meat), **сіль** (salt), and **цу́кор** (sugar) are kitchen words. Ukrainian food vocabulary is a great way to practice these symbols.

toolCalls.description:
Replaces text within a file. By default, the tool expects to find and replace exactly ONE occurrence of `old_string`. If you want to replace multiple occurrences of the exact same string, set `allow_multiple` to true. This tool requires providing significant context around the change to ensure precise targeting. Always use the read_file tool to examine the file's current content before attempting a text replacement.
      
      The user has the ability to modify the `new_string` content. If modified, this will be stated in the response.
      
      Expectation for required parameters:
      1. `old_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace (including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.).
      2. `new_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace `old_string` with (also including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.). Ensure the resulting code is correct and idiomatic and that `old_string` and `new_string` are different.
      3. `instruction` is the detailed instruction of what needs to be changed. It is important to Make it specific and detailed so developers or large language models can understand what needs to be changed and perform the changes on their own if necessary. 
      4. NEVER escape `old_string` or `new_string`, that would break the exact literal text requirement.
      **Important:** If ANY of the above are not satisfied, the tool will fail. CRITICAL for `old_string`: Must uniquely identify the instance(s) to change. Include at least 3 lines of context BEFORE and AFTER the target text, matching whitespace and indentation precisely. If this string matches multiple locations and `allow_multiple` is not true, the tool will fail.
      5. Prefer to break down complex and long changes into multiple smaller atomic calls to this tool. Always check the content of the file after changes or not finding a string to match.
      **Multiple replacements:** Set `allow_multiple` to true if you want to replace ALL occurrences that match `old_string` exactly.

Ось слова, де є апостроф:

- **м'я́со** (meat) — hard **М** + Й + А.
- **п'ять** (five) — hard **П** + Й + А.
- **сім'я́** (family) — hard **М** + Й + А.
- **м'яч** (ball) — hard **М** + Й + А.
- **об'є́кт** (object) — hard **Б** + Й + Е.

> [!note] **Правило апострофа — The apostrophe rule**
> Апостроф — після приголосних **Б**, **П**, **В**, **М**, **Ф**, **Р**. Апостроф — перед **Я**, **Ю**, **Є**, **Ї**. If you see one of these consonants before an iotated vowel, look for the apostrophe.

Чому це важливо? Compare what happens without it versus with it:

- Without it: **М + Я** means «soft М followed by А». The **Й** disappears.
- With it: **М'Я** means «hard М + Й + А». The **Й** is preserved.

Апостроф — це не опція. The apostrophe is not optional. Writing **мясо** instead of **м'ясо** changes the pronunciation entirely. Ukrainian school textbooks drill this from Grade 2. Now you know it too.

<!-- adapted from: Bolshakova, Grade 2 — apostrophe exercises p. 57-58 -->

> [!tip] **Kitchen connection**
> Notice this: **м'ясо** (meat), **сіль** (salt), and **цу́кор** (sugar) are kitchen words. Ukrainian food vocabulary is a great way to practice these symbols.

## Африкати та Ф — Affricates and Ф

Африкати — це особливі приголосні. Affricates are special consonants. They fuse two sounds into one. Your mouth starts in one position. It quickly shifts to another. This happens within a single letter.

### Літера Ц

Start with the sound **Т**. Smoothly transition to **С**. **Ц** fuses them into one sound. Think of English «cats». That is the sound of **Ц**. Your tongue touches the ridge behind your upper teeth. Then it quickly releases into an «s.»

- **цу́кор** (sugar) — an everyday kitchen word.
- **цибу́ля** (onion) — another kitchen staple.

You will often see **Ц** in endings: **-ець**, **-иця**. It is a very productive letter.

### Літера Ч

Схожа на англійський звук "ch". Think of "church". **Ч** is one of the most frequent consonants in Ukrainian.

- **час** (time, hour) — a top 100 word.
- **черепа́ха** (turtle) — a favorite in children's books.
- **чай** (tea) — you will hear this word every day.

> [!practice] **Sound it out**
> Try to pronounce these words. For **Ч**, your tongue presses against the roof. Then it releases with a burst of air.

### Літера Щ

Унікальна українська літера. It contains two sounds: **Ш** + **Ч**. It is uniquely Ukrainian in its pronunciation. Start with the sound «ш». Immediately add «ч.» The result is longer than a simple «sh.»

- **що** (what) — одне з найпопулярніших слів.
- **ще** (still, more) — very common.
- **ща́стя** (happiness) — a beautiful word.

Слово **що** є майже в кожній розмові. You will meet it again and again. Getting its pronunciation right now is a great investment.

> [!warning] **Common mistake**
> Do not replace **Щ** with the sound «ш.» Saying **шо** instead of **що** is a common error. Remember: **Щ** = **Ш** + **Ч**. These are two sounds in one letter.

### Літера Ф

Найпростіша літера в цьому модулі. **Ф** sounds just like English «f.» But it is rare in native Ukrainian words. Most words with **Ф** are borrowings:

- **факт** (fact) — an internationalism.
- **фо́то** (photo) — another familiar borrowing.

**Ф** — це глухий партнер літери **В**. Native Ukrainian words historically preferred **В** or **ХВ**. You will not see **Ф** often. But you should recognize it instantly.

## Диграфи ДЖ, ДЗ — Digraphs

В українській мові є два диграфи. There are two digraphs. These are combinations of two letters for one sound. Do not read them as separate letters. Each digraph is one phoneme.

### ДЖ

Це звук як англійська літера "j". Think of "jungle". **ДЖ** is the voiced partner of **Ч**.

- **джерело́** (spring, source) — a beautiful word.
- **бджола́** (bee) — notice the **Б** before the digraph.

> [!tip] **Remember the pair**
> **Ч** (глухий) ↔ **ДЖ** (дзвінкий). Just like English «ch» ↔ «j.»

### ДЗ

**ДЗ** — унікальний звук. It has no equivalent in English. It is the voiced partner of **Ц**. Think of **Ц** (ts). Then add voicing. Your vocal cords vibrate through the whole sound.

- **дзвін** (bell) — the sound of church bells.
- **дзе́ркало** (mirror) — an everyday object.

> [!culture] **Uniquely Ukrainian**
> Диграф **ДЗ** — це особливість фонетики. The word **дзвін** captures something deeply cultural. It is the sound of church bells across Ukrainian towns. **ДЗ** — це особливий звук.

## Весь алфавіт! — The Full Alphabet Mastered

Now you know all the letters. Here is the complete 33-letter alphabet:

**А Б В Г Ґ Д Е Є Ж З И І Ї Й К Л М Н О П Р С Т У Ф Х Ц Ч Ш Щ Ь Ю Я**

А також диграфи **ДЖ**, **ДЗ** та **апостроф** (**'**).

> [!challenge] **Full-alphabet reading challenge**
> Try to read this text. It uses everything you have learned:

- **Це сім'я́ і м'яч.** (This is a family and a ball.)
- **Джерело́ — це щастя.** (A spring is happiness.)
- **Дзвін — це факт.** (A bell is a fact.)
- **Кінь і бджола́.** (A horse and a bee.)
- **О́сінь. Цу́кор і сіль.** (Autumn. Sugar and salt.)

You did it! You did it! Every word above uses the letters from this module. If you can read them, you can decode any word.

Ось п'ять важливих фраз. Practice reading them aloud:

- **До́брий день!** (Good day!)
- **Як спра́ви?** (How are you?)
- **До́брий ра́нок!** (Good morning!)
- **До́брий ве́чір!** (Good evening!)
- **До поба́чення!** (Goodbye!)

> [!tip] **Фінал! — Celebrate!**
> You can read any word. The reading skills you built are the foundation. From here on, every module will teach you more. Чудова робота! Great job!

## Підсумок — Summary

У цьому модулі — фінал алфавіту:

- **Ь** (м'який знак) — знак для м'яких приголосних. Слова: **сіль**, **день**, **кінь**.
- **Апостроф** (**'**) — розділяє приголосний та голосну. Слова: **м'ясо**, **п'ять**, **сім'я**.
- **Ц, Ч, Щ** — африкати. Слова: **цукор**, **час**, **що**.
- **Ф** — звучить як англійське «f». Слова: **факт**, **фото**.
- **ДЖ, ДЗ** — диграфи. Слова: **джерело**, **дзвін**.

**Self-check — can you answer these?**

1. Що робить м'який знак (Ь)?
2. Після яких приголосних стоїть апостроф?
3. Які звуки утворюють літеру **Щ**?
4. Чому **ДЗ** унікальний?

If you can answer all four, you are ready for Module 5. There you will learn about syllables.

toolCalls.description:
Replaces text within a file. By default, the tool expects to find and replace exactly ONE occurrence of `old_string`. If you want to replace multiple occurrences of the exact same string, set `allow_multiple` to true. This tool requires providing significant context around the change to ensure precise targeting. Always use the read_file tool to examine the file's current content before attempting a text replacement.
      
      The user has the ability to modify the `new_string` content. If modified, this will be stated in the response.
      
      Expectation for required parameters:
      1. `old_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace (including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.).
      2. `new_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace `old_string` with (also including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.). Ensure the resulting code is correct and idiomatic and that `old_string` and `new_string` are different.
      3. `instruction` is the detailed instruction of what needs to be changed. It is important to Make it specific and detailed so developers or large language models can understand what needs to be changed and perform the changes on their own if necessary. 
      4. NEVER escape `old_string` or `new_string`, that would break the exact literal text requirement.
      **Important:** If ANY of the above are not satisfied, the tool will fail. CRITICAL for `old_string`: Must uniquely identify the instance(s) to change. Include at least 3 lines of context BEFORE and AFTER the target text, matching whitespace and indentation precisely. If this string matches multiple locations and `allow_multiple` is not true, the tool will fail.
      5. Prefer to break down complex and long changes into multiple smaller atomic calls to this tool. Always check the content of the file after changes or not finding a string to match.
      **Multiple replacements:** Set `allow_multiple` to true if you want to replace ALL occurrences that match `old_string` exactly.

- **де́нь** (day) — the **Н** is soft.
- **Льві́в** (Lviv) — the **Л** is soft before another consonant.
- **мідь** (copper) — the **Д** is soft.
- **о́сінь** (autumn) — the **Н** is soft.
- **кінь** (horse) — the **Н** is soft.

Look at the rules. **Ь** appears after consonants at the end of a word (**сіль**, **день**). It also appears before another consonant (**Львів**). It never appears at the beginning of a word. It never appears after a vowel.

> [!tip] **Quick win!**
> Here is the greeting **Добрий день!** (Good day!). Now you see why there is a **Ь** at the end. The **Н** is soft. Try to pronounce this. The tip of your tongue touches your palate.

Чому **Ь** важливий? Why is **Ь** important? Look at this minimal pair:

- **кінь** (horse) — soft **Н** at the end.
- **кін** (a stake in a game) — hard **Н** at the end.

Однакові літери, але різне значення. Same letters, but different meaning. The **Ь** changes the quality of the consonant. This creates an entirely different word. Це не просто прикраса. This is not decoration. It is essential.

<!-- adapted from: Kravtsova, Grade 3 — minimal pair exercises for Ь -->

## Апостроф — The Apostrophe

Маленький, але сильний знак. A small but powerful mark. The **apostrophe** (**'**) separates a consonant from a vowel (**Я**, **Ю**, **Є**, **Ї**). This preserves the **Й**-sound. Without it, the sound would be absorbed.

Remember from Module 2. Iotated vowels carry a hidden **Й** at the start: **Я** = Й+А, **Ю** = Й+У, **Є** = Й+Е, **Ї** = Й+І. The apostrophe keeps that **Й** alive.

Ось слова, де є апостроф:

- **м'я́со** (meat) — hard **М** + Й + А.
- **п'ять** (five) — hard **П** + Й + А.
- **сім'я́** (family) — hard **М** + Й + А.
- **м'яч** (ball) — hard **М** + Й + А.
- **об'є́кт** (object) — hard **Б** + Й + Е.

> [!note] **Правило апострофа — The apostrophe rule**
> Апостроф — після приголосних **Б**, **П**, **В**, **М**, **Ф**, **Р**. Апостроф — перед **Я**, **Ю**, **Є**, **Ї**. If you see one of these consonants before an iotated vowel, look for the apostrophe.

Чому це важливо? Compare what happens without it versus with it:

- Without it: **М + Я** means «soft М followed by А». The **Й** disappears.
- With it: **М'Я** means «hard М + Й + А». The **Й** is preserved.

Апостроф — це не опція. The apostrophe is not optional. Writing **мясо** instead of **м'ясо** changes the pronunciation entirely. Ukrainian school textbooks drill this from Grade 2. Now you know it too.

<!-- adapted from: Bolshakova, Grade 2 — apostrophe exercises p. 57-58 -->

> [!tip] **Kitchen connection**
> Notice this: **м'ясо** (meat), **сіль** (salt), and **цу́кор** (sugar) are kitchen words. Ukrainian food vocabulary is a great way to practice these symbols.

## Африкати та Ф — Affricates and Ф

Африкати — це особливі приголосні. Affricates are special consonants. They fuse two sounds into one. Your mouth starts in one position. It quickly shifts to another. This happens within a single letter.

### Літера Ц

Start with the sound **Т**. Smoothly transition to **С**. **Ц** fuses them into one sound. Think of English «cats». That is the sound of **Ц**. Your tongue touches the ridge behind your upper teeth. Then it quickly releases into an «s.»

- **цу́кор** (sugar) — an everyday kitchen word.
- **цибу́ля** (onion) — another kitchen staple.

You will often see **Ц** in endings: **-ець**, **-иця**. It is a very productive letter.

### Літера Ч

Схожа на англійський звук "ch". Think of "church". **Ч** is one of the most frequent consonants in Ukrainian.

- **час** (time, hour) — a top 100 word.
- **черепа́ха** (turtle) — a favorite in children's books.
- **чай** (tea) — you will hear this word every day.

> [!practice] **Sound it out**
> Try to pronounce these words. For **Ч**, your tongue presses against the roof. Then it releases with a burst of air.

### Літера Щ

Унікальна українська літера. It contains two sounds: **Ш** + **Ч**. It is uniquely Ukrainian in its pronunciation. Start with the sound «ш». Immediately add «ч.» The result is longer than a simple «sh.»

- **що** (what) — одне з найпопулярніших слів.
- **ще** (still, more) — very common.
- **ща́стя** (happiness) — a beautiful word.

Слово **що** є майже в кожній розмові. You will meet it again and again. Getting its pronunciation right now is a great investment.

> [!warning] **Common mistake**
> Do not replace **Щ** with the sound «ш.» Saying **шо** instead of **що** is a common error. Remember: **Щ** = **Ш** + **Ч**. These are two sounds in one letter.

### Літера Ф

Найпростіша літера в цьому модулі. **Ф** sounds just like English «f.» But it is rare in native Ukrainian words. Most words with **Ф** are borrowings:

- **факт** (fact) — an internationalism.
- **фо́то** (photo) — another familiar borrowing.

**Ф** — це глухий партнер літери **В**. Native Ukrainian words historically preferred **В** or **ХВ**. You will not see **Ф** often. But you should recognize it instantly.

## Диграфи ДЖ, ДЗ — Digraphs

В українській мові є два диграфи. There are two digraphs. These are combinations of two letters for one sound. Do not read them as separate letters. Each digraph is one phoneme.

### ДЖ

Це звук як англійська літера "j". Think of "jungle". **ДЖ** is the voiced partner of **Ч**.

- **джерело́** (spring, source) — a beautiful word.
- **бджола́** (bee) — notice the **Б** before the digraph.

> [!tip] **Remember the pair**
> **Ч** (глухий) ↔ **ДЖ** (дзвінкий). Just like English «ch» ↔ «j.»

### ДЗ

**ДЗ** — унікальний звук. It has no equivalent in English. It is the voiced partner of **Ц**. Think of **Ц** (ts). Then add voicing. Your vocal cords vibrate through the whole sound.

- **дзвін** (bell) — the sound of church bells.
- **дзе́ркало** (mirror) — an everyday object.

> [!culture] **Uniquely Ukrainian**
> Диграф **ДЗ** — це особливість фонетики. The word **дзвін** captures something deeply cultural. It is the sound of church bells across Ukrainian towns. **ДЗ** — це особливий звук.

## Весь алфавіт! — The Full Alphabet Mastered

Now you know all the letters. Here is the complete 33-letter alphabet:

**А Б В Г Ґ Д Е Є Ж З И І Ї Й К Л М Н О П Р С Т У Ф Х Ц Ч Ш Щ Ь Ю Я**

А також диграфи **ДЖ**, **ДЗ** та **апостроф** (**'**).

> [!challenge] **Full-alphabet reading challenge**
> Try to read this text. It uses everything you have learned:

- **Це сім'я́ і м'яч.** (This is a family and a ball.)
- **Джерело́ — це щастя.** (A spring is happiness.)
- **Дзвін — це факт.** (A bell is a fact.)
- **Кінь і бджола́.** (A horse and a bee.)
- **О́сінь. Цу́кор і сіль.** (Autumn. Sugar and salt.)

You did it! You did it! Every word above uses the letters from this module. If you can read them, you can decode any word.

Ось п'ять важливих фраз. Practice reading them aloud:

- **До́брий день!** (Good day!)
- **Як спра́ви?** (How are you?)
- **До́брий ра́нок!** (Good morning!)
- **До́брий ве́чір!** (Good evening!)
- **До поба́чення!** (Goodbye!)

> [!tip] **Фінал! — Celebrate!**
> You can read any word. The reading skills you built are the foundation. From here on, every module will teach you more. Чудова робота! Great job!

## Підсумок — Summary

У цьому модулі — фінал алфавіту:

- **Ь** (м'який знак) — знак для м'яких приголосних. Слова: **сіль**, **день**, **кінь**.
- **Апостроф** (**'**) — знак між приголосним та голосним. Слова: **м'ясо**, **п'ять**, **сім'я**.
- **Ц, Ч, Щ** — африкати. Слова: **цукор**, **час**, **що**.
- **Ф** — звучить як англійське «f». Слова: **факт**, **фото**.
- **ДЖ, ДЗ** — диграфи. Слова: **джерело**, **дзвін**.

**Self-check — can you answer these?**

1. Що робить м'який знак (Ь)?
2. Після яких приголосних стоїть апостроф?
3. Які звуки утворюють літеру **Щ**?
4. Чому **ДЗ** унікальний?

If you can answer all four, you are ready for Module 5. There you will learn about syllables.

toolCalls.description:
Replaces text within a file. By default, the tool expects to find and replace exactly ONE occurrence of `old_string`. If you want to replace multiple occurrences of the exact same string, set `allow_multiple` to true. This tool requires providing significant context around the change to ensure precise targeting. Always use the read_file tool to examine the file's current content before attempting a text replacement.
      
      The user has the ability to modify the `new_string` content. If modified, this will be stated in the response.
      
      Expectation for required parameters:
      1. `old_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace (including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.).
      2. `new_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace `old_string` with (also including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.). Ensure the resulting code is correct and idiomatic and that `old_string` and `new_string` are different.
      3. `instruction` is the detailed instruction of what needs to be changed. It is important to Make it specific and detailed so developers or large language models can understand what needs to be changed and perform the changes on their own if necessary. 
      4. NEVER escape `old_string` or `new_string`, that would break the exact literal text requirement.
      **Important:** If ANY of the above are not satisfied, the tool will fail. CRITICAL for `old_string`: Must uniquely identify the instance(s) to change. Include at least 3 lines of context BEFORE and AFTER the target text, matching whitespace and indentation precisely. If this string matches multiple locations and `allow_multiple` is not true, the tool will fail.
      5. Prefer to break down complex and long changes into multiple smaller atomic calls to this tool. Always check the content of the file after changes or not finding a string to match.
      **Multiple replacements:** Set `allow_multiple` to true if you want to replace ALL occurrences that match `old_string` exactly.

## Вступ — Introduction

Це великий крок. This is a big step. In Module 1, we mapped out all 33 letters. Module 2 was about vowels. Module 3 focused on consonants. Now, we are ready for the final pieces of the puzzle.

Today you will meet two special symbols. The soft sign **Ь** and the **apostrophe** (**'**). Далі — африкати **Ц**, **Ч**, **Щ**. Next are the affricates **Ц**, **Ч**, **Щ**. These letters pack two sounds into one. Then come the digraphs **ДЖ** and **ДЗ**. These are single sounds written with two letters. Finally, there is the rare letter **Ф**.

Це фінал. This is the finale. After this module, you will be able to read *any* Ukrainian word. Every letter, every symbol, every combination. You will know them all. Let's finish what we started.

## М'який знак — The Soft Sign

Imagine a letter with no sound of its own. The letter **Ь** is exactly that. It only exists to soften (palatalize) the consonant before it. With the letter **Ь**, place your tongue closer to the roof of your mouth. The result is a gentler, «softer» version of the same sound.

Ось кілька слів із **Ь**:

- **сіль** (salt) — the **Л** is soft.
- **де́нь** (day) — the **Н** is soft.
- **Льві́в** (Lviv) — the **Л** is soft before another consonant.
- **мідь** (copper) — the **Д** is soft.
- **о́сінь** (autumn) — the **Н** is soft.
- **кінь** (horse) — the **Н** is soft.

Look at the rules. **Ь** appears after consonants at the end of a word (**сіль**, **день**). It also appears before another consonant (**Львів**). It never appears at the beginning of a word. It never appears after a vowel.

> [!tip] **Quick win!**
> Here is the greeting **Добрий день!** (Good day!). Now you see why there is a **Ь** at the end. The **Н** is soft. Try to pronounce this. The tip of your tongue touches your palate.

Чому **Ь** важливий? Why is **Ь** important? Look at this minimal pair:

- **кінь** (horse) — soft **Н** at the end.
- **кін** (a stake in a game) — hard **Н** at the end.

Однакові літери, але різне значення. Same letters, but different meaning. The **Ь** changes the quality of the consonant. This creates an entirely different word. Це не просто прикраса. This is not decoration. It is essential.

<!-- adapted from: Kravtsova, Grade 3 — minimal pair exercises for Ь -->

## Апостроф — The Apostrophe

Маленький, але сильний знак. A small but powerful mark. The **apostrophe** (**'**) separates a consonant from a vowel (**Я**, **Ю**, **Є**, **Ї**). This preserves the **Й**-sound. Without it, the sound would be absorbed.

Remember from Module 2. Iotated vowels carry a hidden **Й** at the start: **Я** = Й+А, **Ю** = Й+У, **Є** = Й+Е, **Ї** = Й+І. The apostrophe keeps that **Й** alive.

Ось слова, де є апостроф:

- **м'я́со** (meat) — hard **М** + Й + А.
- **п'ять** (five) — hard **П** + Й + А.
- **сім'я́** (family) — hard **М** + Й + А.
- **м'яч** (ball) — hard **М** + Й + А.
- **об'є́кт** (object) — hard **Б** + Й + Е.

> [!note] **Правило апострофа — The apostrophe rule**
> Апостроф — після приголосних **Б**, **П**, **В**, **М**, **Ф**, **Р**. Апостроф — перед **Я**, **Ю**, **Є**, **Ї**. If you see one of these consonants before an iotated vowel, look for the apostrophe.

Чому це важливо? Compare what happens without it versus with it:

- Without it: **М + Я** means «soft М followed by А». The **Й** disappears.
- With it: **М'Я** means «hard М + Й + А». The **Й** is preserved.

Апостроф — це не опція. The apostrophe is not optional. Writing **мясо** instead of **м'ясо** changes the pronunciation entirely. Ukrainian school textbooks drill this from Grade 2. Now you know it too.

<!-- adapted from: Bolshakova, Grade 2 — apostrophe exercises p. 57-58 -->

> [!tip] **Kitchen connection**
> Notice this: **м'ясо** (meat), **сіль** (salt), and **цу́кор** (sugar) are kitchen words. Ukrainian food vocabulary is a great way to practice these symbols.

## Африкати та Ф — Affricates and Ф

Африкати — це особливі приголосні. Affricates are special consonants. They fuse two sounds into one. Your mouth starts in one position. It quickly shifts to another. This happens within a single letter.

### Літера Ц

Start with the sound **Т**. Smoothly transition to **С**. **Ц** fuses them into one sound. Think of English «cats». That is the sound of **Ц**. Your tongue touches the ridge behind your upper teeth. Then it quickly releases into an «s.»

- **цу́кор** (sugar) — an everyday kitchen word.
- **цибу́ля** (onion) — another kitchen staple.

You will often see **Ц** in endings: **-ець**, **-иця**. It is a very productive letter.

### Літера Ч

Схожа на англійський звук "ch". Think of "church". **Ч** is one of the most frequent consonants in Ukrainian.

- **час** (time, hour) — a top 100 word.
- **черепа́ха** (turtle) — a favorite in children's books.
- **чай** (tea) — you will hear this word every day.

> [!practice] **Sound it out**
> Try to pronounce these words. For **Ч**, your tongue presses against the roof. Then it releases with a burst of air.

### Літера Щ

Унікальна українська літера. It contains two sounds: **Ш** + **Ч**. It is uniquely Ukrainian in its pronunciation. Start with the sound «ш». Immediately add «ч.» The result is longer than a simple «sh.»

- **що** (what) — одне з найпопулярніших слів.
- **ще** (still, more) — very common.
- **ща́стя** (happiness) — a beautiful word.

Слово **що** є майже в кожній розмові. You will meet it again and again. Getting its pronunciation right now is a great investment.

> [!warning] **Common mistake**
> Do not replace **Щ** with the sound «ш.» Saying **шо** instead of **що** is a common error. Remember: **Щ** = **Ш** + **Ч**. These are two sounds in one letter.

### Літера Ф

Найпростіша літера в цьому модулі. **Ф** sounds just like English «f.» But it is rare in native Ukrainian words. Most words with **Ф** are borrowings:

- **факт** (fact) — an internationalism.
- **фо́то** (photo) — another familiar borrowing.

**Ф** — це глухий партнер літери **В**. Native Ukrainian words historically preferred **В** or **ХВ**. You will not see **Ф** often. But you should recognize it instantly.

## Диграфи ДЖ, ДЗ — Digraphs

В українській мові є два диграфи. There are two digraphs. These are combinations of two letters for one sound. Do not read them as separate letters. Each digraph is one phoneme.

### ДЖ

Це звук як англійська літера "j". Think of "jungle". **ДЖ** is the voiced partner of **Ч**.

- **джерело́** (spring, source) — a beautiful word.
- **бджола́** (bee) — notice the **Б** before the digraph.

> [!tip] **Remember the pair**
> **Ч** (глухий) ↔ **ДЖ** (дзвінкий). Just like English «ch» ↔ «j.»

### ДЗ

**ДЗ** — унікальний звук. It has no equivalent in English. It is the voiced partner of **Ц**. Think of **Ц** (ts). Then add voicing. Your vocal cords vibrate through the whole sound.

- **дзвін** (bell) — the sound of church bells.
- **дзе́ркало** (mirror) — an everyday object.

> [!culture] **Uniquely Ukrainian**
> Диграф **ДЗ** — це особливість фонетики. The word **дзвін** captures something deeply cultural. It is the sound of church bells across Ukrainian towns. **ДЗ** — це особливий звук.

## Весь алфавіт! — The Full Alphabet Mastered

Now you know all the letters. Here is the complete 33-letter alphabet:

**А Б В Г Ґ Д Е Є Ж З И І Ї Й К Л М Н О П Р С Т У Ф Х Ц Ч Ш Щ Ь Ю Я**

А також диграфи **ДЖ**, **ДЗ** та **апостроф** (**'**).

> [!challenge] **Full-alphabet reading challenge**
> Try to read this text. It uses everything you have learned:

- **Це сім'я́ і м'яч.** (This is a family and a ball.)
- **Джерело́ — це щастя.** (A spring is happiness.)
- **Дзвін — це факт.** (A bell is a fact.)
- **Кінь і бджола́.** (A horse and a bee.)
- **О́сінь. Цу́кор і сіль.** (Autumn. Sugar and salt.)

You did it! You did it! Every word above uses the letters from this module. If you can read them, you can decode any word.

Ось п'ять важливих фраз. Practice reading them aloud:

- **До́брий день!** (Good day!)
- **Як спра́ви?** (How are you?)
- **До́брий ра́нок!** (Good morning!)
- **До́брий ве́чір!** (Good evening!)
- **До поба́чення!** (Goodbye!)

> [!tip] **Фінал! — Celebrate!**
> You can read any word. The reading skills you built are the foundation. From here on, every module will teach you more. Чудова робота! Great job!

## Підсумок — Summary

У цьому модулі — фінал алфавіту:

- **Ь** (м'який знак) — знак для м'яких приголосних. Слова: **сіль**, **день**, **кінь**.
- **Апостроф** (**'**) — знак між приголосним та голосним. Слова: **м'ясо**, **п'ять**, **сім'я**.
- **Ц, Ч, Щ** — африкати. Слова: **цукор**, **час**, **що**.
- **Ф** — звук як англійське «f». Слова: **факт**, **фото**.
- **ДЖ, ДЗ** — диграфи. Слова: **джерело**, **дзвін**.

**Self-check — can you answer these?**

1. Що робить м'який знак (Ь)?
2. Після яких приголосних стоїть апостроф?
3. Які звуки утворюють літеру **Щ**?
4. Чому **ДЗ** унікальний?

If you can answer all four, you are ready for Module 5. There you will learn about syllables.

toolCalls.description:
Replaces text within a file. By default, the tool expects to find and replace exactly ONE occurrence of `old_string`. If you want to replace multiple occurrences of the exact same string, set `allow_multiple` to true. This tool requires providing significant context around the change to ensure precise targeting. Always use the read_file tool to examine the file's current content before attempting a text replacement.
      
      The user has the ability to modify the `new_string` content. If modified, this will be stated in the response.
      
      Expectation for required parameters:
      1. `old_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace (including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.).
      2. `new_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace `old_string` with (also including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.). Ensure the resulting code is correct and idiomatic and that `old_string` and `new_string` are different.
      3. `instruction` is the detailed instruction of what needs to be changed. It is important to Make it specific and detailed so developers or large language models can understand what needs to be changed and perform the changes on their own if necessary. 
      4. NEVER escape `old_string` or `new_string`, that would break the exact literal text requirement.
      **Important:** If ANY of the above are not satisfied, the tool will fail. CRITICAL for `old_string`: Must uniquely identify the instance(s) to change. Include at least 3 lines of context BEFORE and AFTER the target text, matching whitespace and indentation precisely. If this string matches multiple locations and `allow_multiple` is not true, the tool will fail.
      5. Prefer to break down complex and long changes into multiple smaller atomic calls to this tool. Always check the content of the file after changes or not finding a string to match.
      **Multiple replacements:** Set `allow_multiple` to true if you want to replace ALL occurrences that match `old_string` exactly.

toolCalls.name: replace

---
module: a1-004
level: A1
sequence: 4
slug: completing-the-alphabet
version: '2.0'
title: Completing the Alphabet
subtitle: "Кінець алфавіту — Soft Sign, Apostrophe, Affricates, and Digraphs"
focus: grammar
pedagogy: PPP
phase: A1.1 (First Contact)
word_target: 1200
objectives:
- Learner understands the soft sign (Ь) as a consonant modifier with no sound of its own
- Learner understands the apostrophe as a separator preserving the Й-sound
- Learner can pronounce affricates Ц, Ч, Щ and digraphs ДЖ, ДЗ
- Learner knows Ф appears mostly in borrowed words
- Learner can read any Ukrainian word — the full 33-letter alphabet is mastered
---

## Вступ — Introduction

Це великий крок. This is a big step. In Module 1, we mapped out all 33 letters. Module 2 was about vowels. Module 3 focused on consonants. Now, we are ready for the final pieces of the puzzle.

Today you will meet two special symbols. The soft sign **Ь** and the **apostrophe** (**'**). Далі — африкати **Ц**, **Ч**, **Щ**. Next are the affricates **Ц**, **Ч**, **Щ**. These letters pack two sounds into one. Then come the digraphs **ДЖ** and **ДЗ**. These are single sounds written with two letters. Finally, there is the rare letter **Ф**.

Це фінал. This is the finale. After this module, you will be able to read *any* Ukrainian word. Every letter, every symbol, every combination. You will know them all. Let's finish what we started.

## М'який знак — The Soft Sign

Imagine a letter with no sound of its own. The letter **Ь** is exactly that. It only exists to soften (palatalize) the consonant before it. With the letter **Ь**, place your tongue closer to the roof of your mouth. The result is a gentler, «softer» version of the same sound.

Ось кілька слів із **Ь**:

- **сіль** (salt) — the **Л** is soft.
- **де́нь** (day) — the **Н** is soft.
- **Льві́в** (Lviv) — the **Л** is soft before another consonant.
- **мідь** (copper) — the **Д** is soft.
- **о́сінь** (autumn) — the **Н** is soft.
- **кінь** (horse) — the **Н** is soft.

Look at the rules. **Ь** appears after consonants at the end of a word (**сіль**, **день**). It also appears before another consonant (**Львів**). It never appears at the beginning of a word. It never appears after a vowel.

> [!tip] **Quick win!**
> Here is the greeting **Добрий день!** (Good day!). Now you see why there is a **Ь** at the end. The **Н** is soft. Try to pronounce this. The tip of your tongue touches your palate.

Чому **Ь** важливий? Why is **Ь** important? Look at this minimal pair:

- **кінь** (horse) — soft **Н** at the end.
- **кін** (a stake in a game) — hard **Н** at the end.

Однакові літери, але різне значення. Same letters, but different meaning. The **Ь** changes the quality of the consonant. This creates an entirely different word. Це не просто прикраса. This is not decoration. It is essential.

<!-- adapted from: Kravtsova, Grade 3 — minimal pair exercises for Ь -->

## Апостроф — The Apostrophe

Маленький, але сильний знак. A small but powerful mark. The **apostrophe** (**'**) separates a consonant from a vowel (**Я**, **Ю**, **Є**, **Ї**). This preserves the **Й**-sound. Without it, the sound would be absorbed.

Remember from Module 2. Iotated vowels carry a hidden **Й** at the start: **Я** = Й+А, **Ю** = Й+У, **Є** = Й+Е, **Ї** = Й+І. The apostrophe keeps that **Й** alive.

Ось слова, де є апостроф:

- **м'я́со** (meat) — hard **М** + Й + А.
- **п'ять** (five) — hard **П** + Й + А.
- **сім'я́** (family) — hard **М** + Й + А.
- **м'яч** (ball) — hard **М** + Й + А.
- **об'є́кт** (object) — hard **Б** + Й + Е.

> [!note] **Правило апострофа — The apostrophe rule**
> Апостроф — після приголосних **Б**, **П**, **В**, **М**, **Ф**, **Р**. Апостроф — перед **Я**, **Ю**, **Є**, **Ї**. If you see one of these consonants before an iotated vowel, look for the apostrophe.

Чому це важливо? Compare what happens without it versus with it:

- Without it: **М + Я** means «soft М followed by А». The **Й** disappears.
- With it: **М'Я** means «hard М + Й + А». The **Й** is preserved.

Апостроф — це не опція. The apostrophe is not optional. Writing **мясо** instead of **м'ясо** changes the pronunciation entirely. Ukrainian school textbooks drill this from Grade 2. Now you know it too.

<!-- adapted from: Bolshakova, Grade 2 — apostrophe exercises p. 57-58 -->

> [!tip] **Kitchen connection**
> Notice this: **м'ясо** (meat), **сіль** (salt), and **цу́кор** (sugar) are kitchen words. Ukrainian food vocabulary is a great way to practice these symbols.

## Африкати та Ф — Affricates and Ф

Африкати — це особливі приголосні. Affricates are special consonants. They fuse two sounds into one. Your mouth starts in one position. It quickly shifts to another. This happens within a single letter.

### Літера Ц

Start with the sound **Т**. Smoothly transition to **С**. **Ц** fuses them into one sound. Think of English «cats». That is the sound of **Ц**. Your tongue touches the ridge behind your upper teeth. Then it quickly releases into an «s.»

- **цу́кор** (sugar) — an everyday kitchen word.
- **цибу́ля** (onion) — another kitchen staple.

You will often see **Ц** in endings: **-ець**, **-иця**. It is a very productive letter.

### Літера Ч

Схожа на англійський звук "ch". Think of "church". **Ч** is one of the most frequent consonants in Ukrainian.

- **час** (time, hour) — a top 100 word.
- **черепа́ха** (turtle) — a favorite in children's books.
- **чай** (tea) — you will hear this word every day.

> [!practice] **Sound it out**
> Try to pronounce these words. For **Ч**, your tongue presses against the roof. Then it releases with a burst of air.

### Літера Щ

Унікальна українська літера. It contains two sounds: **Ш** + **Ч**. It is uniquely Ukrainian in its pronunciation. Start with the sound «ш». Immediately add «ч.» The result is longer than a simple «sh.»

- **що** (what) — одне з найпопулярніших слів.
- **ще** (still, more) — very common.
- **ща́стя** (happiness) — a beautiful word.

Слово **що** є майже в кожній розмові. You will meet it again and again. Getting its pronunciation right now is a great investment.

> [!warning] **Common mistake**
> Do not replace **Щ** with the sound «ш.» Saying **шо** instead of **що** is a common error. Remember: **Щ** = **Ш** + **Ч**. These are two sounds in one letter.

### Літера Ф

Найпростіша літера в цьому модулі. **Ф** sounds just like English «f.» But it is rare in native Ukrainian words. Most words with **Ф** are borrowings:

- **факт** (fact) — an internationalism.
- **фо́то** (photo) — another familiar borrowing.

**Ф** — це глухий партнер літери **В**. Native Ukrainian words historically preferred **В** or **ХВ**. You will not see **Ф** often. But you should recognize it instantly.

## Диграфи ДЖ, ДЗ — Digraphs

В українській мові є два диграфи. There are two digraphs. These are combinations of two letters for one sound. Do not read them as separate letters. Each digraph is one phoneme.

### ДЖ

Це звук як англійська літера "j". Think of "jungle". **ДЖ** is the voiced partner of **Ч**.

- **джерело́** (spring, source) — a beautiful word.
- **бджола́** (bee) — notice the **Б** before the digraph.

> [!tip] **Remember the pair**
> **Ч** (глухий) ↔ **ДЖ** (дзвінкий). Just like English «ch» ↔ «j.»

### ДЗ

**ДЗ** — унікальний звук. It has no equivalent in English. It is the voiced partner of **Ц**. Think of **Ц** (ts). Then add voicing. Your vocal cords vibrate through the whole sound.

- **дзвін** (bell) — the sound of church bells.
- **дзе́ркало** (mirror) — an everyday object.

> [!culture] **Uniquely Ukrainian**
> Диграф **ДЗ** — це особливість фонетики. The word **дзвін** captures something deeply cultural. It is the sound of church bells across Ukrainian towns. **ДЗ** — це особливий звук.

## Весь алфавіт! — The Full Alphabet Mastered

Now you know all the letters. Here is the complete 33-letter alphabet:

**А Б В Г Ґ Д Е Є Ж З И І Ї Й К Л М Н О П Р С Т У Ф Х Ц Ч Ш Щ Ь Ю Я**

А також диграфи **ДЖ**, **ДЗ** та **апостроф** (**'**).

> [!challenge] **Full-alphabet reading challenge**
> Try to read this text. It uses everything you have learned:

- **Це сім'я́ і м'яч.** (This is a family and a ball.)
- **Джерело́ — це щастя.** (A spring is happiness.)
- **Дзвін — це факт.** (A bell is a fact.)
- **Кінь і бджола́.** (A horse and a bee.)
- **О́сінь. Цу́кор і сіль.** (Autumn. Sugar and salt.)

You did it! You did it! Every word above uses the letters from this module. If you can read them, you can decode any word.

Ось п'ять важливих фраз. Practice reading them aloud:

- **До́брий день!** (Good day!)
- **Як спра́ви?** (How are you?)
- **До́брий ра́нок!** (Good morning!)
- **До́брий ве́чір!** (Good evening!)
- **До поба́чення!** (Goodbye!)

> [!tip] **Фінал! — Celebrate!**
> You can read any word. The reading skills you built are the foundation. From here on, every module will teach you more. Чудова робота! Great job!

## Підсумок — Summary

У цьому модулі — фінал алфавіту:

- **Ь** (м'який знак) — знак для м'яких приголосних. Слова: **сіль**, **день**, **кінь**.
- **Апостроф** (**'**) — знак між приголосним та голосним. Слова: **м'ясо**, **п'ять**, **сім'я**.
- **Ц, Ч, Щ** — африкати. Слова: **цукор**, **час**, **що**.
- **Ф** — звук як англійське «f». Слова: **факт**, **фото**.
- **ДЖ, ДЗ** — диграфи. Слова: **джерело**, **дзвін**.

**Self-check — can you answer these?**

1. Для чого м'який знак (Ь)?
2. Після яких приголосних стоїть апостроф?
3. Які звуки утворюють літеру **Щ**?
4. Чому **ДЗ** унікальний?

If you can answer all four, you are ready for Module 5. There you will learn about syllables.

toolCalls.description:
Replaces text within a file. By default, the tool expects to find and replace exactly ONE occurrence of `old_string`. If you want to replace multiple occurrences of the exact same string, set `allow_multiple` to true. This tool requires providing significant context around the change to ensure precise targeting. Always use the read_file tool to examine the file's current content before attempting a text replacement.
      
      The user has the ability to modify the `new_string` content. If modified, this will be stated in the response.
      
      Expectation for required parameters:
      1. `old_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace (including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.).
      2. `new_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace `old_string` with (also including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.). Ensure the resulting code is correct and idiomatic and that `old_string` and `new_string` are different.
      3. `instruction` is the detailed instruction of what needs to be changed. It is important to Make it specific and detailed so developers or large language models can understand what needs to be changed and perform the changes on their own if necessary. 
      4. NEVER escape `old_string` or `new_string`, that would break the exact literal text requirement.
      **Important:** If ANY of the above are not satisfied, the tool will fail. CRITICAL for `old_string`: Must uniquely identify the instance(s) to change. Include at least 3 lines of context BEFORE and AFTER the target text, matching whitespace and indentation precisely. If this string matches multiple locations and `allow_multiple` is not true, the tool will fail.
      5. Prefer to break down complex and long changes into multiple smaller atomic calls to this tool. Always check the content of the file after changes or not finding a string to match.
      **Multiple replacements:** Set `allow_multiple` to true if you want to replace ALL occurrences that match `old_string` exactly.

toolCalls.name: replace

args.instruction: Replace verb 'утворюють' with a noun phrase.

response.output:
Successfully modified file: /Users/krisztiankoos/projects/learn-ukrainian/curriculum/l2-uk-en/a1/completing-the-alphabet.md (1 replacements). Here is the updated code:
...

**Self-check — can you answer these?**

1. Для чого м'який знак (Ь)?
2. Після яких приголосних апостроф?
3. Які два звуки в літері **Щ**?
4. Чому **ДЗ** унікальний?

If you can answer all four, you are ready for Module 5. There you will learn about syllables.

---
module: a1-004
level: A1
sequence: 4
slug: completing-the-alphabet
version: '2.0'
title: Completing the Alphabet
subtitle: "Кінець алфавіту — Soft Sign, Apostrophe, Affricates, and Digraphs"
focus: grammar
pedagogy: PPP
phase: A1.1 (First Contact)
word_target: 1200
objectives:
- Learner understands the soft sign (Ь) as a consonant modifier with no sound of its own
- Learner understands the apostrophe as a separator preserving the Й-sound
- Learner can pronounce affricates Ц, Ч, Щ and digraphs ДЖ, ДЗ
- Learner knows Ф appears mostly in borrowed words
- Learner can read any Ukrainian word — the full 33-letter alphabet is mastered
---

## Вступ — Introduction

Це великий крок. This is a big step. In Module 1, we mapped out all 33 letters. Module 2 was about vowels. Module 3 focused on consonants. Now, we are ready for the final pieces of the puzzle.

Today you will meet two special symbols. The soft sign **Ь** and the **apostrophe** (**'**). Далі — африкати **Ц**, **Ч**, **Щ**. Next are the affricates **Ц**, **Ч**, **Щ**. These letters pack two sounds into one. Then come the digraphs **ДЖ** and **ДЗ**. These are single sounds written with two letters. Finally, there is the rare letter **Ф**.

Це фінал. This is the finale. After this module, you will be able to read *any* Ukrainian word. Every letter, every symbol, every combination. You will know them all. Let's finish what we started.

## М'який знак — The Soft Sign

Imagine a letter with no sound of its own. The letter **Ь** is exactly that. It only exists to soften (palatalize) the consonant before it. With the letter **Ь**, place your tongue closer to the roof of your mouth. The result is a gentler, «softer» version of the same sound.

Ось кілька слів із **Ь**:

- **сіль** (salt) — the **Л** is soft.
- **де́нь** (day) — the **Н** is soft.
- **Льві́в** (Lviv) — the **Л** is soft before another consonant.
- **мідь** (copper) — the **Д** is soft.
- **о́сінь** (autumn) — the **Н** is soft.
- **кінь** (horse) — the **Н** is soft.

Look at the rules. **Ь** appears after consonants at the end of a word (**сіль**, **день**). It also appears before another consonant (**Львів**). It never appears at the beginning of a word. It never appears after a vowel.

> [!tip] **Quick win!**
> Here is the greeting **Добрий день!** (Good day!). Now you see why there is a **Ь** at the end. The **Н** is soft. Try to pronounce this. The tip of your tongue touches your palate.

Чому **Ь** важливий? Why is **Ь** important? Look at this minimal pair:

- **кінь** (horse) — soft **Н** at the end.
- **кін** (a stake in a game) — hard **Н** at the end.

Однакові літери, але різне значення. Same letters, but different meaning. The **Ь** changes the quality of the consonant. This creates an entirely different word. Це не просто прикраса. This is not decoration. It is essential.

<!-- adapted from: Kravtsova, Grade 3 — minimal pair exercises for Ь -->

## Апостроф — The Apostrophe

Маленький, але сильний знак. A small but powerful mark. The **apostrophe** (**'**) separates a consonant from a vowel (**Я**, **Ю**, **Є**, **Ї**). This preserves the **Й**-sound. Without it, the sound would be absorbed.

Remember from Module 2. Iotated vowels carry a hidden **Й** at the start: **Я** = Й+А, **Ю** = Й+У, **Є** = Й+Е, **Ї** = Й+І. The apostrophe keeps that **Й** alive.

Ось слова, де є апостроф:

- **м'я́со** (meat) — hard **М** + Й + А.
- **п'ять** (five) — hard **П** + Й + А.
- **сім'я́** (family) — hard **М** + Й + А.
- **м'яч** (ball) — hard **М** + Й + А.
- **об'є́кт** (object) — hard **Б** + Й + Е.

> [!note] **Правило апострофа — The apostrophe rule**
> Апостроф — після приголосних **Б**, **П**, **В**, **М**, **Ф**, **Р**. Апостроф — перед **Я**, **Ю**, **Є**, **Ї**. If you see one of these consonants before an iotated vowel, look for the apostrophe.

Чому це важливо? Compare what happens without it versus with it:

- Without it: **М + Я** means «soft М followed by А». The **Й** disappears.
- With it: **М'Я** means «hard М + Й + А». The **Й** is preserved.

Апостроф — це не опція. The apostrophe is not optional. Writing **мясо** instead of **м'ясо** changes the pronunciation entirely. Ukrainian school textbooks drill this from Grade 2. Now you know it too.

<!-- adapted from: Bolshakova, Grade 2 — apostrophe exercises p. 57-58 -->

> [!tip] **Kitchen connection**
> Notice this: **м'ясо** (meat), **сіль** (salt), and **цу́кор** (sugar) are kitchen words. Ukrainian food vocabulary is a great way to practice these symbols.

## Африкати та Ф — Affricates and Ф

Африкати — це особливі приголосні. Affricates are special consonants. They fuse two sounds into one. Your mouth starts in one position. It quickly shifts to another. This happens within a single letter.

### Літера Ц

Start with the sound **Т**. Smoothly transition to **С**. **Ц** fuses them into one sound. Think of English «cats». That is the sound of **Ц**. Your tongue touches the ridge behind your upper teeth. Then it quickly releases into an «s.»

- **цу́кор** (sugar) — an everyday kitchen word.
- **цибу́ля** (onion) — another kitchen staple.

You will often see **Ц** in endings: **-ець**, **-иця**. It is a very productive letter.

### Літера Ч

Схожа на англійський звук "ch". Think of "church". **Ч** is one of the most frequent consonants in Ukrainian.

- **час** (time, hour) — a top 100 word.
- **черепа́ха** (turtle) — a favorite in children's books.
- **чай** (tea) — you will hear this word every day.

> [!practice] **Sound it out**
> Try to pronounce these words. For **Ч**, your tongue presses against the roof. Then it releases with a burst of air.

### Літера Щ

Унікальна українська літера. It contains two sounds: **Ш** + **Ч**. It is uniquely Ukrainian in its pronunciation. Start with the sound «ш». Immediately add «ч.» The result is longer than a simple «sh.»

- **що** (what) — одне з найпопулярніших слів.
- **ще** (still, more) — very common.
- **ща́стя** (happiness) — a beautiful word.

Слово **що** є майже в кожній розмові. You will meet it again and again. Getting its pronunciation right now is a great investment.

> [!warning] **Common mistake**
> Do not replace **Щ** with the sound «ш.» Saying **шо** instead of **що** is a common error. Remember: **Щ** = **Ш** + **Ч**. These are two sounds in one letter.

### Літера Ф

Найпростіша літера в цьому модулі. **Ф** sounds just like English «f.» But it is rare in native Ukrainian words. Most words with **Ф** are borrowings:

- **факт** (fact) — an internationalism.
- **фо́то** (photo) — another familiar borrowing.

**Ф** — це глухий партнер літери **В**. Native Ukrainian words historically preferred **В** or **ХВ**. You will not see **Ф** often. But you should recognize it instantly.

## Диграфи ДЖ, ДЗ — Digraphs

В українській мові є два диграфи. There are two digraphs. These are combinations of two letters for one sound. Do not read them as separate letters. Each digraph is one phoneme.

### ДЖ

Це звук як англійська літера "j". Think of "jungle". **ДЖ** is the voiced partner of **Ч**.

- **джерело́** (spring, source) — a beautiful word.
- **бджола́** (bee) — notice the **Б** before the digraph.

> [!tip] **Remember the pair**
> **Ч** (глухий) ↔ **ДЖ** (дзвінкий). Just like English «ch» ↔ «j.»

### ДЗ

**ДЗ** — унікальний звук. It has no equivalent in English. It is the voiced partner of **Ц**. Think of **Ц** (ts). Then add voicing. Your vocal cords vibrate through the whole sound.

- **дзвін** (bell) — the sound of church bells.
- **дзе́ркало** (mirror) — an everyday object.

> [!culture] **Uniquely Ukrainian**
> Диграф **ДЗ** — це особливість фонетики. The word **дзвін** captures something deeply cultural. It is the sound of church bells across Ukrainian towns. **ДЗ** — це особливий звук.

## Весь алфавіт! — The Full Alphabet Mastered

Now you know all the letters. Here is the complete 33-letter alphabet:

**А Б В Г Ґ Д Е Є Ж З И І Ї Й К Л М Н О П Р С Т У Ф Х Ц Ч Ш Щ Ь Ю Я**

А також диграфи **ДЖ**, **ДЗ** та **апостроф** (**'**).

> [!challenge] **Full-alphabet reading challenge**
> Try to read this text. It uses everything you have learned:

- **Це сім'я́ і м'яч.** (This is a family and a ball.)
- **Джерело́ — це щастя.** (A spring is happiness.)
- **Дзвін — це факт.** (A bell is a fact.)
- **Кінь і бджола́.** (A horse and a bee.)
- **О́сінь. Цу́кор і сіль.** (Autumn. Sugar and salt.)

You did it! You did it! Every word above uses the letters from this module. If you can read them, you can decode any word.

Ось п'ять важливих фраз. Practice reading them aloud:

- **До́брий день!** (Good day!)
- **Як спра́ви?** (How are you?)
- **До́брий ра́нок!** (Good morning!)
- **До́брий ве́чір!** (Good evening!)
- **До поба́чення!** (Goodbye!)

> [!tip] **Фінал! — Celebrate!**
> You can read any word. The reading skills you built are the foundation. From here on, every module will teach you more. Чудова робота! Great job!

## Підсумок — Summary

У цьому модулі — фінал алфавіту:

- **Ь** (м'який знак) — знак для м'яких приголосних. Слова: **сіль**, **день**, **кінь**.
- **Апостроф** (**'**) — знак між приголосним та голосним. Слова: **м'ясо**, **п'ять**, **сім'я**.
- **Ц, Ч, Щ** — африкати. Слова: **цукор**, **час**, **що**.
- **Ф** — звук як англійське «f». Слова: **факт**, **фото**.
- **ДЖ, ДЗ** — диграфи. Слова: **джерело**, **дзвін**.

**Self-check — can you answer these?**

1. Для чого м'який знак (Ь)?
2. Після яких приголосних апостроф?
3. Які звуки утворюють літеру **Щ**?
4. Чому **ДЗ** унікальний?

If you can answer all four, you are ready for Module 5. There you will learn about syllables.

toolCalls.description:
Replaces text within a file. By default, the tool expects to find and replace exactly ONE occurrence of `old_string`. If you want to replace multiple occurrences of the exact same string, set `allow_multiple` to true. This tool requires providing significant context around the change to ensure precise targeting. Always use the read_file tool to examine the file's current content before attempting a text replacement.
      
      The user has the ability to modify the `new_string` content. If modified, this will be stated in the response.
      
      Expectation for required parameters:
      1. `old_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace (including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.).
      2. `new_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace `old_string` with (also including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.). Ensure the resulting code is correct and idiomatic and that `old_string` and `new_string` are different.
      3. `instruction` is the detailed instruction of what needs to be changed. It is important to Make it specific and detailed so developers or large language models can understand what needs to be changed and perform the changes on their own if necessary. 
      4. NEVER escape `old_string` or `new_string`, that would break the exact literal text requirement.
      **Important:** If ANY of the above are not satisfied, the tool will fail. CRITICAL for `old_string`: Must uniquely identify the instance(s) to change. Include at least 3 lines of context BEFORE and AFTER the target text, matching whitespace and indentation precisely. If this string matches multiple locations and `allow_multiple` is not true, the tool will fail.
      5. Prefer to break down complex and long changes into multiple smaller atomic calls to this tool. Always check the content of the file after changes or not finding a string to match.
      **Multiple replacements:** Set `allow_multiple` to true if you want to replace ALL occurrences that match `old_string` exactly.

toolCalls.description:
This tool executes a given shell command as `bash -c <command>`. To run a command in the background, set the `is_background` parameter to true. Do NOT use `&` to background commands. Command is executed as a subprocess that leads its own process group. Command process group can be terminated as `kill -- -PGID` or signaled as `kill -s SIGNAL -- -PGID`.

      Efficiency Guidelines:
      - Quiet Flags: Always prefer silent or quiet flags (e.g., `npm install --silent`, `git --no-pager`) to reduce output volume while still capturing necessary information.
      - Pagination: Always disable terminal pagination to ensure commands terminate (e.g., use `git --no-pager`, `systemctl --no-pager`, or set `PAGER=cat`).

      The following information is returned:

      Output: Combined stdout/stderr. Can be `(empty)` or partial on error and for any unwaited background processes.
      Exit Code: Only included if non-zero (command failed).
      Error: Only included if a process-level error occurred (e.g., spawn failure).
      Signal: Only included if process was terminated by a signal.
      Background PIDs: Only included if background processes were started.
      Process Group PGID: Only included if available.
